sprint.title: Orientation Switch v2: Responsive Reflow (Preserve % Width for Wide Components)
sprint.goal: Fix portrait↔landscape conversion so wide components preserve their relative width (percentage of safe area) and relative positioning. If a component’s width is >60% of the safe area width, it is treated as responsive and reflowed to the new safe width with the same percentage.
definitionOfDone: Orientation switching preserves layout proportions for wide components (>60% safe width)
Both directions work: portrait→landscape and landscape→portrait
Reflow uses safe-area boxes (not full page) and maintains margins/relative positions
Non-wide components keep their absolute size unless they overflow, then clamp rules apply
Text boxes and image frames reflow without breaking crop/aspect rules
Changes are deterministic, reversible, and do not push content outside safe area
Unit + integration tests cover the reflow thresholds and edge cases

stackAssumptions.editorModel: Studio doc with pages + nodes with x/y/w/h (plus crop/style/meta)
stackAssumptions.printModel: Safe area boxes computed from presets for portrait/landscape

backlog.title: Define responsive reflow rules and node classification (wide vs fixed)

definitions.safeBox: the printable safe area rectangle per page (x,y,w,h)

reflowGoals: Preserve relative width for wide nodes: newW = (oldW/safeW_old) * safeW_new
Preserve relative X positioning inside safeBox: newX = safeX_new + ( (oldX-safeX_old) / safeW_old ) * safeW_new
Preserve relative Y positioning using safe height similarly (optional but recommended for symmetry)
constraints: Reflow is based on safeBox, not page box
Do not scale font size in v1 (keep it stable); handle overflow via clamp/warnings

acceptanceTests: Rules doc clearly defines wide vs fixed behavior and formulas
Classification works consistently across node types

backlog.title: Implement core reflow math utilities (percent-based resizing + position mapping)

constraints: No floating drift: round to a consistent precision (e.g., 0.5px or 1px)

acceptanceTests: Relative->absolute mapping round-trips with minimal drift
Clamping keeps nodes inside safe area

backlog.title: Apply responsive reflow during orientation switch (wide nodes preserve % width)

behavior: On orientation change, compute safeBoxOld and safeBoxNew for each page
1) classify wide vs fixed using old safeBox
2) if wide: reflow using relative mapping for x and width (and optionally y/height)
3) if fixed: keep absolute size, but map x/y using relative position (optional) OR keep x/y and clamp

policies.wideNodes: preserve rw (percentage width) and rx position; clamp
policies.fixedNodes: preserve absolute w/h; map x/y proportionally OR keep x/y then clamp (choose one)
policies.recommendedFixedPolicy: map x/y proportionally (keeps layout intent) then clamp

constraints: Must work both directions (portrait→landscape and landscape→portrait)
Must not overwrite user edits; this runs as an orientation transformation on the current doc state

acceptanceTests: A node that was 80% of safe width stays ~80% after switch
A node that was 30% stays ~same pixel width unless it overflows (then clamp)
No nodes end outside safe area after switch

backlog.title: Special handling for text boxes (overflow detection + warnings after reflow)

behavior: After reflow/clamp, re-measure text overflow (client DOM measure)
If overflow: set node.meta.overflow=true and record warning (page warnings)
Do not auto-shrink font in v1 (optional future); keep content stable
constraints: No layout loops; measure once after commit
Warnings must be actionable (click to focus node)

acceptanceTests: Text boxes that overflow after orientation switch get flagged
User can find and fix overflow quickly

backlog.title: Special handling for images/frames (preserve crop + aspect, adjust frame size safely)

behavior: Frame rect reflows like other nodes (wide vs fixed)
Image crop model remains unchanged unless frame aspect changes dramatically
If frame aspect changes, keep crop mode=cover center; do not stretch image

acceptanceTests: Images remain correctly cropped and not distorted after switch

backlog.title: Persist orientation switch result + provide undo/redo integration

behavior: Orientation change is a single undoable operation
Persist updated docJson and orientation preset in Convex

backlog.title: QA + automated tests (threshold behavior, both directions, edge cases)

manualChecklist: Portrait→Landscape: wide component (>=60%) keeps same relative width
Landscape→Portrait: wide component keeps same relative width
Fixed component keeps pixel size but stays inside safe area
Text overflow warnings appear when needed
automation: Unit: relative mapping math round-trip tests
Integration: applyOrientationReflowV2 keeps wide nodes percentage
Integration: clamp ensures inside safe box

acceptanceTests: At least 4 automated tests added and passing
Regression prevents the reported wide-node layout issue

notes: The 60% rule effectively treats wide elements as 'responsive' and preserves their layout intent across aspect changes.
Using safe-area-relative coordinates (rx, rw) avoids drift and keeps print-safe alignment consistent when orientation changes.
